title: 10 best herbal essential oils for low blood pressure
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for low blood pressure are a natural and effective way to regulate blood pressure levels, promoting overall wellness and vitality. These oils work by stimulating circulation, improving oxygenation of the body, and reducing stress and anxiety, which are common causes of low blood pressure. For example, Peppermint oil has been shown to increase peripheral resistance, while Rosemary oil improves circulation and boosts energy levels. By incorporating these oils into daily routines through aromatherapy, massage, or inhalation, individuals with low blood pressure can experience improved mood, increased energy, and a reduced risk of dizziness and fainting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with low blood pressure because it has a natural ability to stimulate and regulate blood flow. The oil contains compounds that help to dilate blood vessels, allowing for increased circulation and improved oxygenation of the body's tissues. Additionally, its mild stimulant properties can help to increase heart rate and blood pressure, providing relief from symptoms associated with hypotension, such as dizziness and fatigue. Overall, maidenhair tree essential oils can be a natural and effective way to support cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic: The aqueous extract of Adiantum capillus-veneris has been shown to exhibit cardiotonic effects, which can help regulate heart rate and blood pressure by improving cardiac contractility and increasing peripheral resistance.
Antioxidant: The plant's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which is associated with cardiovascular disease and low blood pressure.
Vasodilatory: Some studies suggest that Adiantum extracts may have vasodilatory effects, which could help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart and brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maidenhair tree contain compounds that help to improve circulation and lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels.
Barks: The barks of the Maidenhair tree have been traditionally used to treat circulatory problems, including low blood pressure, due to their ability to dilate blood vessels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves at dawn, using scissors or pruning shears for clean cuts, collecting 20-30 grams of fresh material.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area, away from direct sunlight, for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Steam distill the dried ginkgo biloba leaves using a still with a steam capacity of 500 ml, and a condenser temperature of 90°c.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a dark glass vial, filtering it through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove any impurities, yielding 5-7% yield.
Store the freshly extracted ginkgo biloba essential oil in airtight containers, away from direct sunlight and heat sources, for up to 6 months while maintaining its potency and aroma.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng essential oils helps with low blood pressure because it stimulates blood flow and improves circulation, which can help to increase blood pressure back to a healthy range. The oil's natural energizing properties also help to reduce fatigue and weakness associated with low blood pressure, allowing individuals to feel more alert and revitalized. Additionally, ginseng oil's adaptogenic qualities help the body to adapt to stress and promote overall balance, which can further support healthy blood pressure levels.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic property: Ginseng essential oil helps to improve cardiovascular circulation, which can help to increase blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypotension (low blood pressure).
Adaptogenic property: Ginseng essential oil has a balancing effect on the body's response to stress, which can help to stabilize blood pressure fluctuations and promote overall circulatory health.

Vasodilatory property: Ginseng essential oil helps to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and increasing oxygen delivery to tissues, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing low blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginseng plant are rich in bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat low blood pressure by improving circulation and reducing blood vessel constriction.
Rhyzomes: Ginseng rhyzomes contain a unique combination of saponins and flavonoids that help to regulate blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh panax ginseng roots and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steam distill the roots using 100ml of water for every 10 minutes of distillation.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the condensed steam after 30 minutes of distillation.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter or coffee filter to remove impurities.
Store the resulting panax ginseng essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with low blood pressure because it has a natural vasoconstricting effect, which means it causes blood vessels to constrict or narrow. This increases blood flow and blood pressure, helping to alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure such as dizziness and fatigue. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce blood vessel damage and improve overall cardiovascular health, further supporting healthy blood pressure levels.
remedy_properties: Antihypotensive: Herbal licorice essential oil has been shown to have antihypotensive effects, helping to raise blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypotension (low blood pressure). This is due to its ability to stimulate the production of aldosterone, a hormone that helps regulate fluid balance in the body.

Adaptogenic: Herbal licorice essential oil has adaptogenic properties, which means it can help the body adapt to stress and promote balance in physiological processes, including blood pressure regulation. This adaptability can help stabilize blood pressure and mitigate the effects of low blood pressure.

Vasodilatory: The essential oil from herbal licorice root has been found to have vasodilatory properties, which means it can relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, helping to increase blood pressure in individuals with hypotension.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to dilate blood vessels and increase blood flow, making it effective in treating low blood pressure.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been used traditionally to treat circulatory issues, including low blood pressure, due to their ability to stimulate the cardiovascular system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or your own garden measuring 500g.
Clean the harvested roots by gently washing them in cold water and scrubbing off any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm air oven at 60°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried roots in a 1:5 ratio with water for 2 hours using a commercial still to extract essential oil.
Filter and bottle the resulting essential oil, yielding approximately 10ml of glycyrrhiza glabra oil suitable for use in aromatherapy blends.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense essential oils helps with low blood pressure because of its ability to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. The boswellic acids present in frankincense oil have been shown to dilate blood vessels, allowing for increased blood flow to vital organs, which can help raise blood pressure levels. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of frankincense oil may also help to reduce the constriction of blood vessels, further promoting healthy circulation and blood pressure regulation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Frankincense essential oil's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to low blood pressure by protecting blood vessels and promoting healthy circulation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Frankincense essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as boswellic acids, may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help increase blood pressure back to a healthier range.

Vasodilatory properties: Frankincense essential oil has been shown to dilate blood vessels, allowing for increased blood flow and improved circulation, which can help raise blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to stimulate circulation and improve blood flow to combat low blood pressure.
Barks: Contain resinous compounds that help to constrict blood vessels and increase peripheral resistance to raise blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh boswellia serrata leaves at dawn when they are most potent for distillation.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place for 24 hours before commencing distillation.
Combine 200g of dried boswellia serrata leaves with 500ml of water in a stainless steel still for steam distillation.
Distill the mixture at a temperature of 100°c for 2 hours, collecting the essential oil as it condenses.
Filter the resulting oil through a coffee filter to remove impurities and preserve its therapeutic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose essential oils helps with low blood pressure because of its unique properties that stimulate circulation, boost energy levels, and promote cardiovascular health. The oil's natural vasodilatory effects help to dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow and reducing blood pressure. Additionally, damask rose essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce tension in the blood vessels, further contributing to a normalization of blood pressure. This makes it an excellent natural remedy for individuals struggling with low blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Vasoconstrictor properties: Damask rose essential oil helps to constrict blood vessels, which can increase blood pressure and improve circulation, thereby addressing hypotension (low blood pressure).
Antioxidant properties: Damask rose essential oil's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are common underlying causes of low blood pressure.

Cardiovascular tonic properties: The essential oil has been shown to have a positive effect on cardiovascular function, improving circulation, reducing inflammation, and stabilizing heart rate, all of which can help alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots of the damask rose plant have been traditionally used to treat various health issues including low blood pressure due to their ability to improve circulation.

Flowers: The fragrant flowers of the damask rose plant contain a high concentration of essential fatty acids and terpenes, which are believed to help regulate blood pressure and promote cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rose petals at dawn when they are most fragrant and have highest concentration of volatile compounds.
Dry the harvested rose petals in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low setting for 6 hours.
Steam distill dried rose petals with distilled water in a steam distillation apparatus for 4 hours to extract essential oil and rosewater.
Filter the resulting mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the essential oil from the rosewater and discard the solids.
Store the extracted rosa damascena essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and refrigerate at 40°f for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with low blood pressure because of its unique ability to stimulate blood circulation and improve overall cardiovascular health. The oil's active compounds, such as echinacea and flavonoids, help to dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow and oxygen delivery to the brain and other vital organs. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce blood vessel constriction, further supporting healthy blood pressure levels. As a result, using Kansas coneflower essential oils may help individuals with low blood pressure experience improved circulation, reduced fatigue, and increased overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Echinacea pallida's antioxidant compounds may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to improved cardiovascular health and potentially help regulate blood pressure.

Cardiotonic properties: Some studies suggest that extracts of Echinacea pallida may have a positive effect on cardiac function, which could help improve blood circulation and increase blood pressure when it's low.

Adaptogenic properties: Echinacea pallida has been traditionally used as an adaptogen to promote overall health and resilience in response to stress. This adaptogenic effect may help the body respond more effectively to changes in blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help regulate blood flow and pressure.
Flowers: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have vasodilatory properties, which can help lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia roots, carefully digging around the plant without damaging it, and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the harvested echinacea roots in a warm oven at 40°c for 24 hours, or until they become brittle and fragile.
Steam distill the dried echinacea roots using a still or apparatus designed for essential oil extraction, collecting the resulting oil over a period of 4-6 hours.
Filter the distilled essential oil through a coffee filter paper into dark glass bottles, discarding any sediment or impurities that may have formed.
Store the freshly extracted echinacea angustifolia essential oil in a cool, dark place for at least 2 weeks before using it, allowing the oil to mature and stabilize its properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with low blood pressure because of its natural vasodilatory properties, which widen blood vessels and improve circulation. The oil's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, also help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to low blood pressure. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has been shown to increase blood flow to the extremities, improving oxygenation and nutrient delivery to vital organs, helping to stabilize blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Cardiovascular tonic: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil has been shown to improve circulation, increase blood flow, and strengthen the heart, which can help to boost blood pressure in individuals with hypotension.

Vasodilator properties: The oil's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, have a direct effect on blood vessels, causing them to dilate and increasing blood flow, which can help to raise blood pressure.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, both of which are associated with low blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile compounds, the roots are a good source of essential oil that helps to improve circulation and regulate blood pressure.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the Ceylon cinnamon plant contain a higher concentration of active compounds, which can help to dilate blood vessels and lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum bark, ensuring it meets standards for therapeutic-grade use.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm place or using desiccant, taking care not to exceed 30% moisture content.
Steam distill the dried bark using a still or apparatus with a condenser and receiver, processing 10 grams of bark per liter of water.
Monitor the distillation process for 2-3 hours, collecting essential oil in the receiver vessel as it separates from water, resulting in 0.5-1% yield.
Filter and store the collected essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, ready for use or further dilution at a dose of 5-10 drops per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with low blood pressure because of its inherent vasodilatory properties, which aid in widening the blood vessels to improve blood flow. The oil's active compound, gingerol, stimulates the body's natural circulation system, increasing heart rate and blood volume while also relaxing blood vessel walls, leading to a gentle but significant increase in blood pressure. This natural boost helps to counteract the effects of hypotension, promoting overall cardiovascular health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory property: Herbal ginger essential oil has a vasodilatory effect, which helps to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, thus increasing blood pressure back to a healthy range.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to low blood pressure by relaxing blood vessel walls and improving circulation.

Stimulant property: Herbal ginger essential oil has a stimulatory effect on the heart and cardiovascular system, helping to increase cardiac output and peripheral resistance, which can help elevate blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to improve circulation and regulate blood pressure.
Rhyzomes: Contain natural vasodilators that can help to relax blood vessels and increase blood flow, effectively treating low blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh ginger roots from a reputable supplier or your own garden.
Wash the ginger roots thoroughly with lukewarm water and pat dry with paper towels.
Chop the ginger roots into small pieces and blend in a food processor for 2 minutes at high speed.
Steam-distill the blended ginger mixture using 1l of distilled water and a 10% essential oil yield at 100°c for 30 minutes.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle essential oils helps with low blood pressure because it has a profound impact on the cardiovascular system. The oil's active compound, silymarin, has been shown to improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can help to increase blood flow and raise blood pressure levels. Additionally, milk thistle oil's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to low blood pressure. By addressing these underlying factors, milk thistle essential oils may provide a natural remedy for individuals struggling with hypotension.
remedy_properties: Cardioprotective: Milk thistle essential oil has been shown to protect against heart damage and improve cardiovascular health, which can help alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of milk thistle essential oil help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to low blood pressure.
Vasodilatory: Milk thistle essential oil has been found to relax and dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and increasing blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a flavonoid that helps improve circulation and boost blood pressure.
Seeds: Abundant in fatty acids and antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and promote cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality silybum marianum leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated carefully selecting those with no signs of damage or disease.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or mechanical dryers to reduce moisture content to 10% within 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried silybum marianum leaves using a clevenger apparatus or similar equipment at atmospheric pressure for 4-6 hours.
Collect and filter the obtained essential oil through coffee filters or paper towels to remove impurities and sediment taking approximately 30 minutes.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids storing it in a cool place away from direct sunlight within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper essential oils helps with low blood pressure because it stimulates digestion, circulation, and metabolism. The piperine in black pepper oil has been shown to increase vasodilation, improving blood flow and pressure. Additionally, its warming properties help to dilate the blood vessels, allowing more oxygenated blood to reach vital organs. By increasing circulation and improving overall cardiovascular function, black pepper essential oils can help regulate blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Vasoconstrictor: Black pepper essential oil's vasoconstricting property helps to constrict blood vessels, which can increase blood pressure and improve circulation, making it beneficial for individuals with low blood pressure.

Stimulant: The stimulating effect of black pepper essential oil can help to stimulate the nervous system, increasing heart rate and blood pressure, which can be particularly helpful for people who experience sudden drops in blood pressure.

Antioxidant: Black pepper essential oil's antioxidant properties can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to low blood pressure by damaging blood vessels and affecting cardiovascular function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Black pepper roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, which can help regulate blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypotension.
Barks: The barks of the black pepper plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to have vasodilatory properties, making them effective for reducing blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality piper nigrum seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the ground piper nigrum seeds with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a dark glass bottle.
Store the mixture in a cool dry place for 14 days to allow for infusion allowing the herbal properties to transfer to the carrier oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another dark glass bottle discarding the solids and leaving the liquid behind after 1 hour of straining time.
Transfer the infused carrier oil to a clean glass dropper bottle and store it in the refrigerator at 2-8°c for up to 6 months before using as directed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat low blood pressure with herbal essential oils is to blend Rosemary and Ginger essential oils in a carrier oil and apply it topically on the wrists and neck. Alternatively, inhaling the vapors of Peppermint and Eucalyptus essential oils can help increase blood circulation and elevate blood pressure. Consistency is key, so use these blends regularly for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to low blood pressure that are treated with herbal essential oils are circulatory disorders, such as Raynaud's disease and poor circulation. Other conditions that benefit from these oils include hypothyroidism, adrenal fatigue, and stress-related anxiety. Certain essential oils, like peppermint, eucalyptus, and rosemary, help to dilate blood vessels, increase blood flow, and promote relaxation, thereby alleviating symptoms of low blood pressure and related conditions.
